channel.title: KiddieDaily — News for Families
channel.description: Daily kid-friendly news with bias indicators and fact checks. Updated every morning.

item.title: Another new Psyche mission flyby image of Mars
item.description: NASA released a composite image of Mars lit like a crescent as the Psyche mission flew by for a gravity assist. Watch a stunning time lapse of the whole flyby. The post Another new Psyche mission flyb — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Science word of the day: Cryptology
item.description: Pronunciation: Krip-TAH'-leh-jee — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: New Species of Monkey Found in DR Congo Shows How Much There’s Left to Discover
item.description: When news reaches the public that a new species has been identified, the chances really are 9 times out of 10 that it’s some deep sea slug or a spider. From the Congo comes the story of the exce — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Josh Kerr wants to run the fastest mile ever. This is the shoe Brooks built to help him do it.
item.description: We spoke with Danny Orr, senior director of product development at Brooks, to find out what it takes to build a track spike that can handle running a mile at more than 16 mph. The post Josh Kerr wants — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: A quasar breaks the record for most distant supermassive black hole
item.description: The Euclid space telescope discovery could help researchers understand how black holes grew so massive so quickly in the early universe. — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: James Webb telescope uses trippy Einstein prediction to probe the farthest reaches of the universe — Space photo of the week
item.description: The James Webb telescope peers through a warped bubble of space-time to study galaxies from the first billion years after the Big Bang. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: ‘Dead’ coral reef found alive and thriving off West Africa
item.description: The long-lost mesophotic ecosystem near Benin may be part of an even bigger formation. The post ‘Dead’ coral reef found alive and thriving off West Africa appeared first on Popular Science — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Provides Sweeping View of Broom Point
item.description: Description This view looking back up at the outside lip of the 490-foot-tall (150-meter-tall) rim of Jezero Crater was taken by the Mastcam-Z instrument aboard NASA’s Perseverance on May 15, 2025, th — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: 'It's absolutely exciting!': Astronomers discover an atmosphere around an Earth-like planet in the habitable zone for first time ever
item.description: Astronomers have detected helium escaping from the super-Earth LHS 1140 b, providing the strongest evidence yet that a rocky planet in another star's habitable zone has retained an atmosphere for bill — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Actively Steerable Femtosat Constellations for In-situ Exploration of Saturn’s Rings, Atmosphere, and Magnetosphere
item.description: Michael RubensteinNorthwestern University, Chicago We propose a mission to use ~10,000 actively steerable femtosats to map the ring composition, atmospheric composition and density, and the magnetic f — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: This “exotic weirdo” exoplanet has a rocky surface and an atmosphere
item.description: Astronomers found a helium atmosphere around a rocky exoplanet — a good sign for the search for life outside the solar system. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Gedenkstein Melitta Bentz in Dresden, Germany
item.description: Melitta Bentz liked to drink coffee. But at the beginning of the 20th century, coffee was not prepared as it is today. When enjoying coffee, the coffee grounds in her mouth and the somewhat bitter aft — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Sir Alex Ferguson pays tribute to former rival Kevin Keegan
item.description: Former Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson says Kevin Keegan "carried the love of the game on his sleeve" as he paid tribute to his former rival. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
item.description: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government. — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: King cobras may be riding trains across India [COMIC]
item.description: The snakes may be tempted onboard by tasty rodents — but train rides can carry cobras far from their forest habitats. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Reigning champion Argentina escapes with remarkable World Cup victory over Egypt
item.description: Egypt was leading late, up 2-0. The Argentinians looked beaten. But they fought and fought and fought. Scoring one goal, then another to equalize. And, finally, a third to advance to the quarterfinal. — 6 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: NASA's Hubble spots a stellar sparkler for the Fourth of July
item.description: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured a spectacular red, white, and blue view of one of the Milky Way's oldest star clusters to celebrate the nation's 250th anniversary. Hidden within the ancient — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Scientists say Beefalo are all beef, no -alo. Breeders disagree
item.description: A whole-genome analysis of Beefalo, a hybrid bison-cattle breed, suggests very few individuals have any bison DNA at all, a new study reports. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Strange Spheres Washed Ashore on an Australian Beach. Authorities Say They're Probably 'Space Balls'—Empty Fuel Vessels From a Rocket
item.description: The shiny objects—each roughly twice the size of a basketball—began to appear on July 3. Authorities initially worried that they were hazardous but have since determined that they're likely safe bits — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover featuring his personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026, on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. For 2 — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.
